question:
Which of the following statements is incorrect?
(I) Peripheral vascular bundles are generally smaller than the centrally located ones.
(II) Water-containing cavities are present within the vascular bundles.
(III) In grasses, certain abaxial epidermal cells along the veins modify themselves into large, empty, colourless cells.
(IV) The parallel venation in monocot leaves is reflected in the nearly similar sizes of vascular bundles.
explanation: III is incorrect. In grasses, certain adaxial epidermal cells along the veins modify themselves into large, empty, colourless cells.
subject: Biology

question:
Which of the following statements are correct about a dicot leaf?
(I) The mesophyll is differentiated into palisade parenchyma and spongy parenchyma.
(II) The adaxially placed palisade parenchyma.
(III) The palisade is made up of elongated cells, which are arranged vertically and parallel to each other.
(IV) The spongy parenchyma is oval or round and tightly arranged.
(V) There are numerous large spaces and air cavities between spongy parenchyma cells.
explanation: I, II, III, V are correct. The incorrect statements can be corrected as the spongy parenchyma is oval or round and loosely arranged.

question:

(VI) The vascular bundles are surrounded by a layer of thick-walled bundle sheath cells.
explanation: II, III, IV, V, VI are correct . The incorrect statements can be corrected as peripheral vascular bundles are generally smaller than the centrally located ones in monocot stem.

explanation: In the given figure of T.S of dicot root, the parts marked as A, B, C, D, E and F are respectively cortex, epiblema, pith, endodermis, root hairs and pericycle. This arrangement keeps the xylem bundles in direct contact with the outer tissue of the roots which conduct water absorbed by root hairs to the inside. Xylem is exarch.
